provenance_note: Generated from the first-battlefield inspection manifest plus repo-inspection rules and git metadata so the portal can expose source references, recent implementation changes, and a bounded set of repo-derived criterion states.

model.label: First Battlefield Readiness
model.goal_statement: Build a goal-directed polymer/material discovery system that interprets user-specified material requirements, asks clarifying questions when critical scientific constraints are missing, searches and organizes evidence, returns the best-supported material answer when current evidence is sufficient, and only requests additional data or experiments when the evidence is insufficient.
model.scoring_note: Readiness is computed from weighted criteria inside each capability. Hard battlefield gates prevent the page from confusing broad architectural progress with trustworthy first-battlefield readiness.

families.label: Goal Understanding
families.description: Interpret the incoming request as a bounded polymer/material discovery objective instead of a generic prompt or upload summary.
families.target_state: The system consistently treats the user request as a first-battlefield material goal with stable target semantics and session continuity.
families.why_it_matters: If the goal is not interpreted as a bounded discovery objective, every downstream scientific judgment becomes unstable.

children.label: Goal intake and persistence
children.description: Capture the raw goal, structure it, and keep it available across session/workspace continuity.
children.target_state: Every first-battlefield goal is persisted as a first-class record with enough provenance to drive later scientific steps.
children.implementation_basis: MaterialGoalSpecification is persisted per session/workspace and fed into later projection and workbench shaping.
children.current_evidence: Goal specifications, requirement sufficiency state, and clarification questions already survive session reopen.
children.limitation: Revision-to-revision goal provenance is still lighter than the rest of the scientific-state model.
children.blocker: The system still lacks a richer audit trail for how a goal evolves after multiple clarifications.
children.recommended_next_step: Add stronger goal-revision framing so later answer decisions can compare current intent versus earlier intent.
children.why_it_matters: This is the anchor for every later coverage, retrieval, and answer decision step.

criteria.label: Structured goal record exists
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system persists a first-class material goal record rather than only raw text.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found a first-class MaterialGoalSpecification contract plus goal-intake shaping that writes requirement status into the structured goal.
criteria.gap: Historical goal revision lineage is still not first-class.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: MaterialGoalSpecification contract exists (system/scientific_state/contracts.py)
Goal service writes requirement sufficiency into the structured record (system/services/material_goal_service.py)

criteria.label: Goal survives session reopen
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether reopening a session restores the structured goal context.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found material-goal projection shaping plus a session-reopen test covering the first-battlefield projection path.
criteria.gap: Continuity is still recomputed rather than historically versioned.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Projection service still builds a material-goal summary (system/services/scientific_session_projection_service.py)
Projection continuity test covers session reopen (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)

criteria.label: Goal provenance depth
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system preserves a rich explanation of goal revision history.
criteria.current_evidence: Current continuity preserves the active structured goal but not yet a deeper revision graph.
criteria.gap: Goal revision lineage is still shallow.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Polymer/material domain bounding
children.description: Bound the first battlefield to polymer/material discovery and avoid fake target-agnostic behavior.
children.target_state: The system reliably keeps the goal inside the fixed polymer/material battlefield while honestly surfacing bridge-state limits.
children.implementation_basis: The intake path is explicitly scoped to polymer_material and the broader doctrine is now visible in the workbench and docs.
children.current_evidence: The battlefield-specific goal path is now explicitly named and carried through the current system surfaces.
children.limitation: Domain semantics are still bridge-state and do not yet express richer chemistry/material ontologies.
children.blocker: Complex material intents can still be flattened into broad requirement phrases.
children.recommended_next_step: Strengthen first-battlefield material semantics without claiming unsupported target-agnostic reach.
children.why_it_matters: Truthful domain bounding protects the scientific core from unsupported generalization.

criteria.label: Explicit battlefield scope is enforced
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the path explicitly constrains itself to the first battlefield.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit polymer_material scope in both the goal service and the persisted contract shape.
criteria.gap: Subdomain semantics are still bounded and bridge-state.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Goal service fixes the domain scope to polymer_material (system/services/material_goal_service.py)
Material-goal contract defaults to polymer_material (system/scientific_state/contracts.py)

criteria.label: Target function normalization
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the goal is converted into a bounded target material function rather than left as free text.
criteria.current_evidence: Target material function is represented in the current structured goal shape.
criteria.gap: Normalization still depends on a bounded semantic vocabulary.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Bridge-state honesty is surfaced
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the surface is explicit about what remains bridge-state or legacy-constrained.
criteria.current_evidence: The current doctrine and inspection framing openly describe bridge-state limitations.
criteria.gap: This is still curated doctrine rather than auto-derived runtime annotation.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

families.label: Requirement Structuring
families.description: Turn the raw material goal into explicit scientific requirement dimensions that later evidence can be tested against.
families.target_state: The system can represent the user’s material requirements as explicit, inspectable, and sufficiently complete decision dimensions.
families.why_it_matters: Without structured requirements, evidence matching and answer sufficiency judgment are not scientifically grounded.

children.label: Structured requirement dimensions
children.description: Extract target properties, environment, lifecycle, trigger, safety, and function dimensions into a structured goal shape.
children.target_state: Every goal exposes the first-battlefield requirement dimensions in a stable structure that can be covered or left unknown.
children.implementation_basis: MaterialGoalSpecification already carries named requirement groups for properties, environment, lifecycle, triggers, safety, and target function.
children.current_evidence: These dimensions are already threaded into retrieval, support trace, coverage, and answer decision logic.
children.limitation: Dimension semantics are still template-bounded rather than a richer material ontology.
children.blocker: Synonym handling and deeper requirement semantics remain limited.
children.recommended_next_step: Strengthen dimension normalization so coverage can rely less on direct term overlap.
children.why_it_matters: Coverage and support tracing need explicit requirement slots rather than a free-form request blob.

criteria.label: Major requirement dimensions are present
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the goal model exposes the major first-battlefield dimensions.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit first-battlefield requirement dimensions in the support-trace layer.
criteria.gap: Richer second-order material semantics are still not present.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Support trace config includes desired properties (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace config includes operating environment (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace config includes lifecycle (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace config includes target material function (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)

criteria.label: Dimensions propagate into downstream services
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the structured dimensions are actually consumed downstream.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found the dimension structure propagating through retrieval, support trace, coverage, and answer decision layers.
criteria.gap: Propagation still depends on bridge-state matching semantics rather than a richer ontology.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Retrieval consumes structured goal dimensions (system/services/material_goal_retrieval_service.py)
Support trace iterates the dimension set (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Coverage is built from the support trace (system/services/material_goal_coverage_service.py)
Answer service consumes coverage output (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Dimension semantics are richer than keywords
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether dimensions capture deeper semantics beyond templated phrases.
criteria.current_evidence: The structure exists, but interpretation is still conservative and bounded.
criteria.gap: Richer material semantics and uncertainty representation are still missing.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Critical requirement completeness
children.description: Judge whether the current goal is sufficiently specified to proceed toward retrieval and answer logic.
children.target_state: The system reliably distinguishes between sufficiently specified and scientifically incomplete goals before answer work begins.
children.implementation_basis: Requirement sufficiency is already computed during intake and blocks later progression when critical constraints are absent.
children.current_evidence: The system now refuses to proceed to answer logic when the goal remains insufficiently specified.
children.limitation: Criticality logic is still bridge-state and can miss nuanced insufficiencies.
children.blocker: The system still lacks richer prioritization of which missing requirement most distorts the answer boundary.
children.recommended_next_step: Expand criticality rules using the same disciplined first-battlefield requirement structure.
children.why_it_matters: Answer sufficiency is meaningless if the goal itself is not sufficiently specified.

criteria.label: Sufficiency status is explicit
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the goal path explicitly records requirement sufficiency status.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit requirement sufficiency computation and storage in the material-goal path.
criteria.gap: Finer-grained insufficiency semantics remain limited.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Goal service computes sufficiency status explicitly (system/services/material_goal_service.py)
Contract stores requirement status explicitly (system/scientific_state/contracts.py)

criteria.label: Critical gaps block downstream flow
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether insufficient goals are prevented from being treated as answer-ready.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found both source logic and tests confirming that insufficient goals do not flow into answer promotion.
criteria.gap: Blocking logic is still bounded to current first-battlefield semantics.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Test covers retrieval blocking on insufficient goals (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)
Answer service requires sufficiently specified goals (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Missing constraints are prioritized by answer impact
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system ranks missing requirements by how much they affect answer trustworthiness.
criteria.current_evidence: Clarifications are targeted, but their ranking is still relatively static.
criteria.gap: No stronger answer-impact ranking yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

families.label: Clarification / Missing Constraint Detection
families.description: Ask for missing scientific constraints when the goal is incomplete, rather than pretending an answer is already possible.
families.target_state: The system asks bounded, decision-relevant clarification questions exactly where missing requirements still block scientific trustworthiness.
families.why_it_matters: The first battlefield requires the system to ask clarifying questions only when critical scientific constraints are missing.

children.label: Missing constraint detection
children.description: Detect missing environment, lifecycle, trigger, and safety constraints that materially affect answerability.
children.target_state: The system recognizes the major first-battlefield missing-constraint patterns and exposes them before retrieval or answer promotion.
children.implementation_basis: The intake layer already generates clarification questions when environment, lifecycle, trigger, or safety constraints are missing.
children.current_evidence: Current goal intake explicitly detects missing environment and other key requirement families.
children.limitation: Detection is still rule-driven and can miss more subtle ambiguity patterns.
children.blocker: Nuanced ambiguity is still flattened into broad missing-constraint categories.
children.recommended_next_step: Expand bounded ambiguity detection while preserving conservative behavior.
children.why_it_matters: This keeps the system from mistaking underspecified intent for answerable intent.

criteria.label: Environment gaps are detected
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether environment omissions trigger a clarification path.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found environment-sensitive missing-constraint and clarification logic in the goal service.
criteria.gap: Environment semantics remain bounded and phrase-driven.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Goal service contains environment-sensitive missing-constraint logic (system/services/material_goal_service.py)
Goal service builds clarification questions from missing constraints (system/services/material_goal_service.py)

criteria.label: Lifecycle, trigger, and safety gaps are detected
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether other critical scientific constraints are explicitly detected when absent.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit lifecycle, trigger, and safety gap categories in the first-battlefield support-trace path.
criteria.gap: Detection remains bounded and still depends on current dimension semantics.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Lifecycle gap code exists (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Trigger gap code exists (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Safety/regulatory gap code exists (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)

criteria.label: Subtle ambiguity detection
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system catches nuanced incompleteness beyond obvious missing fields.
criteria.current_evidence: The current path goes beyond one missing field, but it is still bounded.
criteria.gap: More subtle scientific ambiguity remains only partly handled.

children.label: Prioritized clarification generation
children.description: Generate the next most useful clarification question instead of dumping broad forms of generic caution.
children.target_state: Clarification requests are ranked by how much they reduce first-battlefield answer uncertainty.
children.implementation_basis: The current goal layer already generates targeted clarification prompts when critical constraints are missing.
children.current_evidence: Clarification output is bounded and domain-shaped instead of generic.
children.limitation: Questions are still relatively template-driven rather than ranked by downstream leverage.
children.blocker: The system cannot yet compare which clarification would most increase answer readiness.
children.recommended_next_step: Add answer-impact ranking so clarification output becomes more strategically ordered.
children.why_it_matters: Good clarification is part of discovery steering, not just form completion.

criteria.label: Clarification questions are bounded and domain-specific
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether clarification output stays within the first battlefield rather than generic assistant behavior.
criteria.current_evidence: The current prompts are targeted to material-goal requirement gaps.
criteria.gap: Prompt ranking remains limited.

criteria.label: Clarifications are ranked by answer impact
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system knows which missing constraint matters most to the answer boundary.
criteria.current_evidence: Current questions are targeted but not deeply ranked by readiness leverage.
criteria.gap: No stronger answer-impact model yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: The system avoids unnecessary clarification
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system asks only the missing questions that materially matter.
criteria.current_evidence: The current path is bounded, but its selectivity is still heuristic.
criteria.gap: Clarification precision is still evolving.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

families.label: Evidence Retrieval and Organization
families.description: Search and organize grounded support for candidate material directions without fabricating evidence depth that does not exist.
families.target_state: The system can retrieve, organize, and bound real evidence against the structured material goal, with enough depth to support later answer decisions.
families.why_it_matters: This is the main bridge-state bottleneck between a capable product workbench and a real discovery engine.

children.label: Grounded evidence matching
children.description: Map the structured goal onto grounded evidence already available in the current scientific/session context.
children.target_state: The system can retrieve evidence with enough grounding depth to support or refute requirement dimensions, not just match terms.
children.implementation_basis: Retrieval now maps structured goal requirements onto existing session evidence, model outputs, and recommendation records.
children.current_evidence: The system already distinguishes no_grounded_evidence, weak_partial_evidence, and candidate_directions_available.
children.limitation: Retrieval is still session-local and relatively term-and-summary driven.
children.blocker: Thin evidence retrieval remains the biggest barrier to a trustworthy first-battlefield answer.
children.recommended_next_step: Increase grounding depth and richer evidence matching before widening any answer claims.
children.why_it_matters: Answer sufficiency cannot become strong while evidence retrieval remains thin and shallow.

battlefield_gate.rationale: The system cannot honestly claim first-battlefield readiness until retrieval grounding is stronger than session-local term matching.

criteria.label: Retrieval sufficiency states are explicit
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether retrieval distinguishes no evidence, weak evidence, and candidate directions.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit retrieval sufficiency states in the retrieval service.
criteria.gap: These states still summarize a relatively thin evidence basis.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Retrieval emits no_grounded_evidence (system/services/material_goal_retrieval_service.py)
Retrieval emits weak_partial_evidence (system/services/material_goal_retrieval_service.py)
Retrieval emits candidate_directions_available (system/services/material_goal_retrieval_service.py)

criteria.label: Grounding depth goes beyond thin summary matching
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether retrieved evidence is rich enough to support dimension-level scientific judgments.
criteria.current_evidence: Grounding is real, but still bounded to current session traces and support summaries.
criteria.gap: No deeper evidence corpus or richer evidence linking yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Thin retrieval is surfaced honestly
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether weak retrieval is exposed as weak rather than promoted.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found tests explicitly covering weak-partial retrieval surfacing and answer withholding.
criteria.gap: This honesty still depends on the upstream retrieval layer staying disciplined.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Tests cover weak_partial_evidence surfacing (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)
Tests cover no fabricated answer from weak partial evidence (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)

children.label: Candidate material direction organization
children.description: Organize retrieved support into conservative candidate material directions with visible support boundaries.
children.target_state: Candidate directions are stable, inspectable, and sufficiently differentiated to support later coverage and answer work.
children.implementation_basis: Retrieval already groups matched support into conservative candidate directions with support strength and limitation lines.
children.current_evidence: The workbench now shows candidate material directions rather than pretending to output a final answer directly.
children.limitation: Direction identity and aggregation are still bridge-state and can remain coarse.
children.blocker: Material direction identity is still not rich enough to fully support high-trust selection among close candidates.
children.recommended_next_step: Improve direction identity and support aggregation without sliding into fabricated precision.
children.why_it_matters: Coverage and answer decision need a coherent leading direction before they can judge sufficiency.

criteria.label: Candidate directions are assembled explicitly

criteria.current_evidence: Retrieval now returns organized candidate directions instead of only isolated evidence snippets.
criteria.gap: Direction identity is still relatively coarse.

criteria.label: Direction support boundaries remain visible

criteria.current_evidence: Support strength and limitation lines already accompany the candidate directions.
criteria.gap: Support provenance is still summary-shaped.

criteria.label: Candidate directions are comparatively differentiable
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the current evidence basis supports a strong comparison between directions.
criteria.current_evidence: Direction organization exists, but it remains thin where retrieval is thin.

families.label: Requirement Coverage and Support Traceability
families.description: Trace each requirement dimension to explicit support basis, contradiction basis, and missing-evidence basis so answer decisions become inspectable.
families.target_state: Every critical requirement dimension can be traced to what supports it, what contradicts it, and what remains missing.
families.why_it_matters: This is the core shift from retrieval summaries toward a more legible scientific decision substrate.

children.label: Per-dimension support trace
children.description: Attach exact matched support lines, observed versus indirect support, contradiction indicators, uncovered terms, and gap codes to each requirement dimension.
children.target_state: Each requirement dimension exposes a compact but explicit support trace that can be audited by the user and reused by later logic.
children.implementation_basis: MaterialGoalSupportTrace now derives explicit per-dimension support records for the leading direction.
children.current_evidence: Support traces now show matched lines, observed lines, indirect lines, contradiction indicators, uncovered terms, and gap codes.
children.limitation: Support traces are still constrained by the quality of current retrieval support lines.
children.blocker: Traceability cannot become stronger than the evidentiary richness of the retrieval layer beneath it.
children.recommended_next_step: Increase direct evidence provenance and dimension-level grounding beneath the trace layer.
children.why_it_matters: This is the mechanism that lets the system answer: what supports this requirement, what contradicts it, and what is still missing?

battlefield_gate.rationale: The system cannot be battlefield-ready while critical requirement support still depends on shallow, weakly attributable traces.

criteria.label: Support basis is explicit per dimension
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the trace explicitly lists matched support and uncovered terms per requirement dimension.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit support, uncovered-term, and contradiction fields inside the support-trace service output.
criteria.gap: Trace provenance is still limited by the richness of the underlying evidence lines.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.

Support trace records uncovered terms (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace records contradiction indicators (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)

criteria.label: Observed versus indirect support is distinguished
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system separates stronger observed support from indirect support.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit observed-versus-indirect separation in the support-trace service.
criteria.gap: The distinction is explicit, but the evidence basis itself remains bridge-state.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Support trace separates observed evidence lines (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace preserves indirect support lines (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)

criteria.label: Trace provenance is rich enough for stronger audit
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the trace can show deeper evidence provenance beyond support-line summaries.
criteria.current_evidence: Traceability has become explicit, but its provenance remains bounded.
criteria.gap: A richer evidence-to-requirement link model is still missing.

children.label: Requirement coverage judgment
children.description: Convert support traces into explicit per-dimension coverage judgments such as supported, partially supported, unknown, or contradicted.
children.target_state: Coverage expresses whether each requirement dimension is truly supported enough to participate in answer promotion.
children.implementation_basis: MaterialGoalCoverage now depends on support traces rather than direct coarse term checks.
children.current_evidence: Discovery surfaces now expose requirement dimensions, status per dimension, rationale, and gaps.
children.limitation: Coverage status still inherits retrieval and contradiction coarseness where the underlying evidence is weak.
children.blocker: Coverage is more inspectable now, but still not backed by a deeper evidence graph.
children.recommended_next_step: Strengthen dimension semantics and contradiction attribution while keeping coverage conservative.
children.why_it_matters: Answer promotion now depends on coverage, so this layer must stay explicit and disciplined.

criteria.label: Coverage is derived from the support trace
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether coverage status now depends on explicit trace structure rather than a shallow summary.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found coverage explicitly consuming the support trace as its primary substrate.
criteria.gap: Coverage still inherits bridge-state limits from the trace beneath it.

Coverage derives status from trace items (system/services/material_goal_coverage_service.py)

criteria.label: Critical dimensions can block answer promotion
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether uncovered or contradicted critical dimensions are visible and decision-relevant.
criteria.current_evidence: Critical requirement dimensions now drive answer withholding when under-supported.
criteria.gap: Dimension criticality is still partly encoded by current field structure.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Coverage captures deeper requirement semantics
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether coverage can express nuanced dimension semantics beyond term coverage.
criteria.current_evidence: Coverage has moved beyond raw term checks, but the semantic depth is still bounded.
criteria.gap: Richer requirement semantics remain to be built.

families.label: Contradiction Handling and Unknown Detection
families.description: Treat contradiction pressure and explicit unknowns as first-class reasons not to return an answer prematurely.
families.target_state: The system can say not just that a dimension is weak, but whether it is unsupported, indirectly supported, contradicted, or still missing decisive evidence.
families.why_it_matters: A truth-seeking discovery system must know why not to trust apparent support.

children.label: Dimension-aware contradiction attribution
children.description: Map contradiction indicators to the requirement dimensions they pressure, while honestly marking when the attribution is still coarse.
children.target_state: Contradictions can be linked to specific requirement dimensions whenever the current scientific state genuinely supports that precision.
children.implementation_basis: Support traces now attempt dimension-aware contradiction attribution and surface when attribution is still coarse.
children.current_evidence: The current system now exposes when contradiction pressure is dimension-specific versus direction-level.
children.limitation: Contradiction objects are still not inherently dimension-tagged, so attribution remains partly coarse.
children.blocker: Without stronger dimension tagging, contradiction handling still caps answer trustworthiness.
children.recommended_next_step: Carry stronger provenance and dimension tags into contradiction records and support traces.
children.why_it_matters: Contradiction handling is one of the main barriers between plausible support and trustworthy support.

battlefield_gate.rationale: The system cannot honestly claim battlefield readiness while contradiction pressure remains only partly attributable to the dimensions it may invalidate.

criteria.label: Contradictions can be linked into support traces
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether contradiction indicators can be surfaced inside the per-dimension trace layer.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit dimension contradiction attribution logic plus provenance about whether attribution is dimension-specific or coarse.
criteria.gap: Contradiction objects are still not richly dimension-tagged.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Support trace contains dimension contradiction attribution logic (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Support trace provenance distinguishes contradiction attribution mode (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)

criteria.label: Dimension-specific attribution is available when justified
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system can attach contradiction pressure to the correct requirement dimension.
criteria.current_evidence: Some dimension-aware mapping is now present, but it is still cautious and limited.
criteria.gap: Dimension tags are not yet first-class in contradiction records.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Contradiction provenance is rich enough for strong blocking
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether contradiction evidence is deep enough to support stronger dimension-level blocking logic.
criteria.current_evidence: Current contradiction provenance remains coarser than the new support trace model.
criteria.gap: Evidence-level contradiction attribution remains largely unbuilt.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Requirement-linked explicit unknowns
children.description: Derive explicit unknowns from uncovered dimensions, indirect-only support, sparse evidence, and unresolved contradiction pressure.
children.target_state: Every insufficient case exposes the concrete requirement-linked unknowns that still prevent a trustworthy answer.
children.implementation_basis: Unknowns now derive from support-trace and coverage gaps rather than vague caution language.
children.current_evidence: Insufficient cases now surface codes like sparse_evidence_basis, weak_environmental_match, and unresolved_contradiction_pressure.
children.limitation: The unknown ontology is still first-version and intentionally bounded.
children.blocker: Unknowns are now explicit, but they are not yet deeply typed or prioritized by experimental value.
children.recommended_next_step: Expand the unknown ontology and tie it more directly to experiment-closing value.
children.why_it_matters: Unknowns are the bridge between honest withholding and useful next-step guidance.

criteria.label: Unknowns are derived from uncovered critical dimensions
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the unknowns correspond to real gaps in the current requirement coverage.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found explicit derivation of unknowns from coverage and support-trace structure.
criteria.gap: Unknown typing and prioritization are still first-version.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Answer service derives unknowns from trace/coverage structure (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)
Answer service starts unknowns from coverage critical unknowns (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Unknowns remain machine-readable and inspectable
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system exposes explicit unknown categories rather than only prose summaries.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found machine-readable gap codes and structured answer-gap request payloads in the current source.
criteria.gap: The ontology remains intentionally bounded.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Support trace emits machine-readable gap codes (system/services/material_goal_support_trace_service.py)
Answer request payload keeps structured provenance (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Unknowns are prioritized by closure value
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system knows which unknown is most important to close first.
criteria.current_evidence: The system now narrows the unknown list, but prioritization is still coarse.
criteria.gap: No stronger value-of-information logic yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

families.label: Answer Sufficiency Judgment and Follow-up Actioning
families.description: Make the central battlefield decision: answer now when support is sufficient, or withhold and ask only for the data that would close the insufficiency.
families.target_state: The system returns a best-supported material answer only when coverage, support trace, contradiction handling, and evidence grounding justify it.
families.why_it_matters: This is the fixed first-battlefield decision boundary around which the current architecture is being rebuilt.

children.label: Bounded answer promotion
children.description: Promote a best-supported answer only when critical requirement dimensions are actually covered and contradiction pressure is not blocking.
children.target_state: Answer promotion depends on explicit coverage and support sufficiency, not shallow retrieval optimism.
children.implementation_basis: MaterialGoalAnswerDecision now uses coverage as its primary gate and still retains conservative secondary guards.
children.current_evidence: Answer promotion now requires supported critical dimensions and blocks weak/partial retrieval from masquerading as a supported answer.
children.limitation: The logic is stronger than before, but upstream evidence depth still caps how often a trustworthy answer can be returned.
children.blocker: The answer boundary is now meaningful, but the evidence basis beneath it is still not battlefield-strong.
children.recommended_next_step: Keep the answer policy conservative while deepening the support basis beneath it.
children.why_it_matters: This is where the system stops being a recommendation shell and starts acting like a disciplined discovery engine.

battlefield_gate.rationale: Battlefield readiness requires a real, reliable answer boundary; this capability must stay strong and remain grounded in explicit support.

criteria.label: Coverage is the primary answer gate
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the answer decision primarily relies on structured coverage rather than raw retrieval heuristics.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found answer logic explicitly gating on critical coverage outcomes.
criteria.gap: The gate is meaningful now, but upstream evidence depth still limits answerability.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Answer service accepts explicit coverage input (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)
Answer service gates on critical coverage status (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Weak or partial support does not become a supported answer
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether weak retrieval or partial critical coverage is explicitly withheld.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found tests explicitly covering answer withholding for weak evidence and uncovered critical dimensions.
criteria.gap: Withholding is only as strong as the upstream traces that feed it.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Tests cover no fabricated answer from weak partial evidence (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)
Tests cover critical-dimension blocking (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)

criteria.label: Answer promotion is justified dimension by dimension
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system can explain why each critical dimension passed the gate.
criteria.current_evidence: Coverage and support trace give a more legible basis now, but not yet a deeper evidence graph.
criteria.gap: Justification is explicit but still bridge-state.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Insufficiency follow-up actioning
children.description: When the answer is insufficient, request only the next data or experiment steps that directly close the explicit insufficiency.
children.target_state: The system requests bounded additional data or experiments only because the current evidence basis is insufficient, not because generic caution language exists.
children.implementation_basis: Insufficient cases already emit bounded additional-data and experiment requests linked to trace and coverage gaps.
children.current_evidence: The current answer layer now requests more only in insufficient cases and ties follow-up to explicit insufficiency reasons.
children.limitation: Follow-up specificity is still first-version and constrained by the current unknown ontology.
children.blocker: Experiment request quality is still limited by the granularity of the unknown model.
children.recommended_next_step: Improve follow-up prioritization and experimental specificity from the explicit unknown structure.
children.why_it_matters: The first battlefield requires not just withholding, but disciplined next-step steering when current evidence is insufficient.

battlefield_gate.rationale: Battlefield readiness requires the system to ask for more only when the evidence basis is insufficient and to do so in a requirement-linked way.

criteria.label: Additional requests appear only when the answer is insufficient
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether additional data or experiments are requested only in insufficient cases.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found tests covering the boundary between no requests in supported cases and requests in insufficient cases.
criteria.gap: Request specificity and prioritization remain first-version.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Tests assert no extra data requests when an answer is supportable (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)
Tests assert no experiment requests when an answer is supportable (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)
Tests assert requests appear in insufficient cases (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)

criteria.label: Requests are linked to explicit insufficiency gaps
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether each request can be traced back to a support, coverage, or contradiction gap.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection could not confirm linked-unknown request shaping in the current answer service source.
criteria.gap: Follow-up requests may no longer be tightly coupled to the triggering insufficiency.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection could not confirm the full expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Answer service emits contradiction-linked experiment requests (system/services/material_goal_answer_service.py)

criteria.label: Requests are prioritized by closure value
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system knows which follow-up request gives the highest leverage toward answer sufficiency.
criteria.current_evidence: Current requests are bounded, but prioritization quality is still early-stage.
criteria.gap: No stronger closure-value model yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

families.label: Session Continuity and Inspection Surfaces
families.description: Keep the scientific state legible across session reopen, Discovery workbench surfaces, and the portal-facing inspection layer.
families.target_state: The system can reopen, project, and inspect first-battlefield state without losing the scientific basis of its current answer boundary.
families.why_it_matters: A discovery system is only operationally useful if its reasoning survives reopen and remains inspectable.

children.label: Session reopen continuity
children.description: Recompute and project first-battlefield state on session reopen without adding unstable persistence pressure.
children.target_state: Goal specification, retrieval, support trace, coverage, and answer decision all survive reopen via truthful recomputation.
children.implementation_basis: Projection/workbench continuity already recomputes the first-battlefield layers on reopen.
children.current_evidence: Support trace, coverage, and answer decision are all exposed again when a session is reopened.
children.limitation: This continuity is recomputed rather than historically versioned.
children.blocker: The system cannot yet compare how a prior answer boundary differed from the current one after policy changes.
children.recommended_next_step: Add historical comparison framing once the read-model policy is stable enough to justify it.
children.why_it_matters: The instrument must show the real current scientific state, not a detached page-specific interpretation.

criteria.label: Projection includes the first-battlefield layers
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether reopen projection includes the current first-battlefield state stack.
criteria.current_evidence: Repo inspection found the projected first-battlefield state stack in source and in projection tests.
criteria.gap: Historical diffing across policy revisions is still limited.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: Repo inspection confirmed the expected source footprint for this criterion.
repo_inspection_evidence: Projection includes support trace (system/services/scientific_session_projection_service.py)
Projection includes coverage (system/services/scientific_session_projection_service.py)
Projection includes answer decision (system/services/scientific_session_projection_service.py)
Tests assert answer decision is projected (tests/test_scientific_session_projection.py)

criteria.label: Recomputed read models stay continuity-safe
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether recomputed read models preserve reopen continuity without new persistence.
criteria.current_evidence: The new first-battlefield layers are intentionally recomputed and survive reopen.
criteria.gap: Version-to-version comparison remains limited.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Historical diffs across policy evolution remain legible
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system can explain how the current read-model differs from earlier policy versions.
criteria.current_evidence: Current projection continuity is strong, but historical comparison remains only partly present.
criteria.gap: No dedicated historical readiness diff yet.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Multi-level inspection surface
children.description: Expose the scientific state in layered inspection surfaces so users can move from overview to evidence-linked detail.
children.target_state: The workbench and portal provide an inspection path from overview to capability to criterion to dependency/gap basis.
children.implementation_basis: Discovery already exposes retrieval, support trace, coverage, and answer panels, and this portal page provides a readiness inspection surface.
children.current_evidence: The page already offers graph, matrix, blockers, next moves, and selection-driven detail views.
children.limitation: The inspection surface still depends on curated modeled state rather than auto-derived runtime instrumentation.
children.blocker: Keeping increasing scientific detail readable without flattening it into decorative summaries remains an active design challenge.
children.recommended_next_step: Continue deepening audit detail and keep linked interactions disciplined as the underlying scientific state grows.
children.why_it_matters: A strategically useful inspection page has to reveal structure, relationships, and blockers rather than only status labels.

criteria.gap: This still reflects the generated inspection snapshot, not live runtime telemetry.

families.label: Epistemic Conservatism
families.description: Preserve the discipline that prevents the system from overclaiming scientific capability or answer support.
families.target_state: Every visible answer, withholding decision, and readiness judgment stays bounded by what the current system can actually support.
families.why_it_matters: A battlefield-ready system must be trustworthy, not just rich in surfaces or heuristics.

children.label: Answer restraint
children.description: Prevent weak, partial, or contradicted evidence from being promoted into a best-supported material answer.
children.target_state: The system consistently under-answers rather than overclaiming when evidence is weak or contradictions remain unresolved.
children.implementation_basis: Coverage-based gating and explicit insufficiency states now block unsupported answer promotion.
children.current_evidence: Thin retrieval and partial critical coverage are now surfaced honestly rather than as optimistic answers.
children.limitation: Restraint is only as strong as the fidelity of the upstream traces and contradiction handling.
children.blocker: Pressure to look complete can still exceed what the evidence basis truly justifies.
children.recommended_next_step: Keep every new capability anchored in explicit support before broadening any answer claims.
children.why_it_matters: Restraint keeps the system scientifically credible while other layers mature.

criteria.label: Thin retrieval is not promoted
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the system refuses to turn weak retrieval into a supported answer.
criteria.current_evidence: Weak/partial retrieval already blocks answer promotion.
criteria.gap: Still depends on upstream retrieval discipline.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Critical unsupported dimensions block answers
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether critical dimensions can stop the system from returning an answer.
criteria.current_evidence: Coverage now blocks answer promotion when critical requirement support is insufficient.
criteria.gap: Coverage semantics are still evolving.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: The page/system remains explicit about bridge-state limits
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the surfaced narrative stays honest about current limits.
criteria.current_evidence: The current docs, workbench, and inspection layer all speak in bounded bridge-state terms.
criteria.gap: Some parts of the broader product shell still carry legacy framing.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

children.label: Inspectable withholding
children.description: Explain not just that the system is withholding, but exactly why it is withholding and what would close the insufficiency.
children.target_state: Insufficient cases expose explicit unknowns, contradiction pressure, gap codes, and bounded next-step requests in a way users can inspect.
children.implementation_basis: Support trace, coverage, and answer decision already expose explicit unknowns, insufficiency reasons, and follow-up requests.
children.current_evidence: The current answer layer is much better at saying why it cannot answer yet and what is still missing.
children.limitation: The insufficiency vocabulary is still evolving and not yet a deep ontology.
children.blocker: Withholding is inspectable, but the precision of the explanation remains bounded by the current trace layer.
children.recommended_next_step: Deepen support and contradiction provenance so inspectable withholding becomes even more specific.
children.why_it_matters: This is what turns honest restraint into operationally useful scientific guidance.

criteria.label: Unknowns are explained explicitly
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether insufficient cases expose requirement-linked unknowns rather than vague summaries.
criteria.current_evidence: Unknowns and insufficiency reasons are already visible and machine-readable.
criteria.gap: The ontology is still first-version.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: Next-step requests are tied to gaps
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether additional data or experiments are explicitly tied to the blocking gaps.
criteria.current_evidence: Current follow-up requests are linked to explicit insufficiency causes.
criteria.gap: Request specificity is still bounded.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.

criteria.label: The withholding rationale has strong audit depth
criteria.measurement_basis: Check whether the user can trace the withholding reason through the scientific layers.
criteria.current_evidence: Audit depth is much stronger than before, but still limited by the current modeled traces.
criteria.gap: Deeper runtime-derived evidence trace links remain future work.
criteria.repo_inspection_summary: This criterion is currently seeded from the inspection manifest and not yet repo-inspected.
